cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. So the problem is asking between what times he could have gone. The key here is to look at all the times he was seen doing other things and figure out when he wasn't occupied, right? First, let me list out all the known activities and their times. Andrew woke up at 5am. Then, William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So from 5 to 6, he's playing tennis. Then Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. Wait, that's 6 to 9. Then Tiffany saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. So 9 to 11. Then John saw him waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. That's a long time, 11am to 5pm. Then Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. And the clothing store was closed after 9pm. So now, we need to find the time intervals where Andrew wasn't doing any of these activities. Let me map out the timeline: - 5am to 6am: tennis - 6am to 9am: deli (lunch) - 9am to 11am: photos at Pisa - 11am to 5pm: train station - 6pm to 9pm: library So the times when he wasn't doing these activities would be the gaps between these activities. Let's check: After waking up at 5am, he immediately starts playing tennis. So from 5-6, he's occupied. Then from 6-9, he's at the deli. Then 9-11 at Pisa. Then 11am-5pm at the train station. Then 6-9pm at the library. So the gaps between these would be: - Before 5am: but he woke up at 5am, so maybe he didn't go before that. But the store's closing time is after 9pm, but he was at the library until 9pm. So what about between 5pm and 6pm? Because after the train station from 11am to 5pm, the next activity is 6pm at the library. So between 5pm and 6pm, he's not accounted for. Also, between 5am and 6am, he's playing tennis. But before 5am, he was asleep, so probably not going anywhere. So the possible free time is 5pm to 6pm. Now, the clothing store is closed after 9pm. So if he went to the store, he would have to do it before 9pm. But during 5pm to 6pm, he's free. Let me check the options. Looking at the options: (A) 9am to 11am: he was taking photos, so no. (B) 6pm to 9pm: he was at the library, so no. (C) 11am to 5pm: he was at the train station, so no. (D) 5pm to 6pm: that's the only time he's free. So
